content:
Thunderbird does not reject passwords that will be the server you are attempting to connect to which is objecting to something being wrong.

It could be the password is wrong or password is correct but not for the other server settings. More info is needed.

What version of Thunderbird are you using ? What server are you trying to get a connection ? gmail ? yahoo ? aol ? etc What server settings are you using ? You could simply upload images that show Account Settings > Server Settings and just scrub out the email address/username.

It would also be helpful if you could upload an image of the error message.

In the meantime please check to see if you have recently enabled a VPN. Some Anti-Virus now provide a VPN and in several cases I've come across people who did not realise it was there let alone enabled. Please switch it off as many server do not like it as it hides your real IP address and server may think you are a hacker - so it blocks access.

Firewall Access Firewall which may the defaul on computer or one controlled by an Anti-Virus. Thunderbird program must be set up as an allowed program to ensure nothing gets blocked.

content: A simple approach is to 1) increase the size of the cache so that compacting is less frequent, and 2) when the prompt appears, wait until the mailboxes update.
creator: davidsk

content:
Thank you for your reply. However, it does not address the root cause of the problem. Your first suggestion will indeed cause the compacting to occur less frequently, but logically cause the time to compact to increase with the increase in cache size. Option 2 doesn't really help since simply waiting for all of the mailboxes to update while the interface is inaccessible while the compact window has the focus doesn't allow truly knowing when that is done. I have waited what seemed to be enough time (several minutes) only to be told that compacting can't be completed because the a mailbox is still updating. The suggested approaches have been done but the root cause or the problem isn't resolved.

So, the question I originally asked concerns user control over the timing of the mailbox compacting. So to restate: is there a way for the user to delay the compacting process, or have Thunderbird not initiate it until it is safe to do so to begin with?
creator: palotta

content:
PAlotta said
It would seem like a simple matter to either give the user a way to delay the compacting process, or have Thunderbird not initiate it until it is safe to do so to begin with.

Decent ideas, for which I have filed bug reports. But so far no bites.

You're seeing two different issues, one of which is the potential compact savings is incorrect, resulting in constant compacts on startup. This issue is rare, but is real and has resisted definition to the point of being able to fix it.

You could increase the value as Rick suggests but inevitable compacts will again happen on startup. And so, unfortunately the only way to avoid this is to disable automatic compact, and periodically do the maintenance compacts manually yourself.

content:
There is no option in Thunderbird's settings to hide those 'replied' and/or 'forwarded' arrows. The only way is to put the following CSS code in a userChrome.css file: 

/*  Remove Arrows in subject column */ tr[data-properties~="replied"], tr[data-properties~="forwarded"] { display: none !important; }


• Copy-paste the above lines in a TextEdit window, save the file, name it 'userChrome.css' and remove the .txt extension your Mac will add automatically to the name. • In the Finder create a new folder, name it 'chrome' and move the userChrome.css file in chrome • in TB go to Settings > General > scroll down to the bottom, click 'Config-Editor', search for the preference toolkit.legacyUserProfileCustomizations.stylesheets and toggle its value from false to true • in TB go to Help > Troubleshooting Information > Profile Folder > Show in Finder.... then quit TB • move the chrome folder in the profile folder and restart TB

content: To change the mailnews_wraplength to 0, do this - click settings>general - scroll to bottom right where a link to 'config editor' appears. click that - enter mailnews_wraplength and thunderbird will locate the line for you - change the value to 0 and save - i suggest reopening the config editor to verify that your change took. - you may need to close and restart thunderbird to see the change take effect.

content:
You don't give us much to go on. What version of Thunderbird? Who is your email provider? Are you using IMAP or POP3? When you go to your provider's web mail site, do you see all messages there?

My first guess is that you have inadvertently activated one of the Quick Filters and it's hiding messages from view based on Unread status, Starred status, Contact or Tags. Here's an article describing how that works:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/quick-filter-toolbar
